response.justification: This candidate brings exactly the right baseline—hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework work at re:cinq demonstrates deep familiarity with agentic systems. The Dunnhumby role shows strong distributed systems expertise (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency optimization, async I/O). However, the absence of Python is a material gap for a must-have skill, and incident response capabilities aren't clearly evidenced. Resume also contains geographic inconsistencies (Berlin location but all roles listed as Kenya). Interview should explore Python competency, on-call experience, and resume accuracy.
strengths: Direct hands-on experience building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks in current role—exactly the agentic system work the position expects
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems expertise from Dunnhumby role handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency optimization, async I/O, and caching strategies
Strong systems thinking across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems; proven ability to architect and optimize complex technical solutions
concerns: No Python experience visible in resume or skills section, despite 'strong Python' being a must-have technical requirement
Incident response and on-call operational experience not explicitly demonstrated in resume despite role requiring 'out-of-hours on-call rotation'
Geographic inconsistencies in resume (Berlin listed as location but all employment roles shown as Kenya) raise credibility questions and need clarification

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems expertise (millions of RPS, low-latency optimization)
Current hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and agentic workflow experience
